instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Is PF Withdrawal Taxable for NRI?
When Indians settle or work abroad, one common concern is whether their savings in the Provident Fund are still safe and easily accessible. The question most people ask is, “Is PF withdrawal taxable for NRI?” The answer isn’t always straightforward.
Tax rules depend on factors like how many years you worked in India, when you choose to withdraw, and whether your PAN and other details are updated with the EPFO. Knowing these rules can help NRIs avoid unexpected deductions and manage their retirement savings wisely.
Understanding NRI PF Withdrawal
When an individual working in India moves abroad and becomes a Non-Resident Indian (NRI), their contributions to the Employees’ Provident Fund (EPF) stop, since the EPF Act applies only to employees working in India. The EPF account balance, which includes both employer and employee contributions and interest that has built up, is safely invested with the EPFO and can be withdrawn under certain circumstances.
For most employees, EPF withdrawals are permitted at retirement age (58 years), upon retirement, or after being unemployed for more than two months. But in the case of NRIs relocating abroad permanently, the rules are more flexible. They are allowed to withdraw the entire EPF balance immediately after moving overseas, regardless of age or employment status. This makes it easier for professionals settling abroad to access their retirement savings without waiting until the standard retirement age.
Withdrawal can be done online through the UAN Member Portal or UMANG App if Aadhaar is linked to UAN. For those who prefer the offline route, forms can be submitted directly to the EPFO office, though employer endorsement is required if UAN is not Aadhaar-linked. In both cases, supporting documents like PAN, Aadhaar, proof of exit from the last employment, and bank details are essential.
Eligibility Criteria for PF Withdrawal by NRIs
NRIs can withdraw their Provident Fund (PF) under specific circumstances, depending on whether the move abroad is temporary or permanent.
Temporary Relocation (SSA Countries)
If you are posted to a country that has a Social Security Agreement (SSA) with India, you can apply for a Certificate of Coverage (CoC) from EPFO. This exempts you from social security contributions in the host country while your PF account in India continues, provided your employer keeps contributing to the scheme.
Temporary Relocation (Non-SSA Countries)
If you move to a country without an SSA, you will need to contribute to both the host country’s system and continue your PF contributions in India.
Permanent Relocation Abroad
If you move overseas permanently, you can withdraw your entire PF corpus immediately. To do this, your KYC must be completed on the UAN portal (Aadhaar, PAN, bank details, mobile number), your PF balance should be consolidated into the last active account, and your employer must update your exit date. The withdrawn amount will be credited to your Indian savings or NRO account.
Taxability of PF Withdrawal for NRIs
For NRIs, the tax treatment of Provident Fund withdrawals depends largely on the length of service. Withdrawals made after completing 5 years of service are exempt from tax. However, if you withdraw before completing 5 years of service, there would be a TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) on the same. The TDS rules are as follows –
Withdrawal amount is below ₹50,000 – No TDS applicable
Withdrawal amount is more than ₹50,000 – TDS of 10% is applicable with PAN and 30% without PAN
To prevent double taxation, NRIs can avail benefits under the Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement (DTAA) between India and their country of residence.
Non-resident members should also note that a surcharge and 4% cess are added to the TDS. The surcharge varies by income: nil up to ₹50 lakh, 10% from ₹50 lakh to ₹1 crore, 15% from ₹1 crore to ₹2 crore, 25% from ₹2 crore to ₹5 crore, and 37% above ₹5 crore.
Key Takeaways
If you become an NRI, you can withdraw your EPF balance immediately after your residential status changes.
If you are going abroad temporarily for work, you can keep your EPF account activated.
If you are temporarily relocating to a country having an SSA with India, you would be exempted from contributing to the foreign country’s social security scheme.
For non-SSA countries, you have to contribute to your EPF account in India and also to the social security scheme of the country you move to.
For NRIs, PF withdrawal is taxable if done before completing five years of continuous service.
Withdrawals made after five years are tax-free in India.
A TDS is also applicable on withdrawals made before 5 years if the amount is more than ₹50,000
NRIs can use DTAA provisions to reduce their tax liability or claim credit in their country of residence.
Conclusion
Managing Provident Fund withdrawals as an NRI requires a clear understanding of both eligibility and taxation rules. Withdrawals made after five years of service remain tax-free, but early withdrawals attract TDS and may increase your tax burden if not planned carefully.
By ensuring your KYC is updated, PAN is linked, and DTAA benefits are properly claimed, you can minimise deductions and maximise the value of your savings. With proper planning, your PF corpus can serve as a strong financial cushion even after you move abroad.
FAQs
Can NRIs claim tax exemptions on PF withdrawal under DTAA?
Yes. Under Section 90 of the Income Tax Act, NRIs can claim benefits under the Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement (DTAA) between India and their country of residence.
What is the TDS rate on PF withdrawal for NRIs?
For NRIs, the TDS rate on Provident Fund withdrawal depends on the length of service and PAN status. If the withdrawal is made before completing five years of continuous service, TDS is deducted at 10% if the PAN is updated and 30% if it isn’t.
On the other hand, if the PF is withdrawn after five years of service, no TDS is applied.
How can NRIs avoid TDS on PF withdrawal?
TDS is applicable only on withdrawals made before 5 years, and the amount is more than ₹50,000. For lower amounts and if the withdrawal is after 5 years of service, no TDS is applicable.
What documents are required to claim tax benefits on PF withdrawal?
Here are the documents that are needed to claim tax benefits on PF withdrawal -
PAN Card
Aadhaar
UAN details
Bank account details (Indian savings or NRO)
Proof of last employment exit
Passport and visa copies
Tax Residency Certificate (TRC)
Form 10F
Self-declaration of residency status
Does the amount of PF withdrawal affect its taxability for NRIs?
Yes, the amount of PF withdrawal is important for TDS implications. A TDS is applicable if you withdraw the EPF before completing 5 years of service and if the amount is more than ₹50,000.
Disclaimer

While reasonable care has been taken in compiling the information, Bajaj Life Insurance Limited (Formerly known as Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Limited) assumes no liability for its accuracy. The opinions expressed do not constitute formal recommendations, professional advice, or definitive guidance. Any illustrations included are for conceptual clarity only and do not reflect the actual performance of any product or offering.
Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility

output.final_text:
Is PF Withdrawal Taxable for NRI?
When Indians settle or work abroad, one common concern is whether their savings in the Provident Fund are still safe and easily accessible. The question most people ask is, “Is PF withdrawal taxable for NRI?” The answer isn’t always straightforward.
Tax rules depend on factors like how many years you worked in India, when you choose to withdraw the PF Balance, and whether your PAN and other details are updated with the EPFO (Employees’ Provident Fund Organisation). Knowing these rules can help NRIs avoid unexpected deductions and manage their retirement savings wisely.
Understanding NRI PF Withdrawal
When an individual working in India moves abroad, meets the specified conditions and becomes a Non-Resident Indian (NRI), their contributions to the Employees’ Provident Fund (EPF) stop, since the EPF Act applies only to employees working in India. The EPF account balance, which includes both employer and employee contributions and interest that has built up, stays with the EPFO and can be withdrawn under certain circumstances.
For most employees, EPF withdrawals are permitted in three instances –
 At retirement age (after age 58 )  Upon retirement, or  After being unemployed for more than two months.
But in the case of NRIs relocating abroad permanently, the rules are more flexible. They are allowed to withdraw the entire EPF balance immediately after moving overseas, regardless of age or employment status1. This makes it easier for professionals settling abroad to access their retirement savings without waiting until the standard retirement age.
Withdrawal can be done online through the UAN (Universal Account Number)Member Portal or UMANG App if Aadhaar is linked to UAN. For those who prefer the offline route, forms can be submitted directly to the EPFO office, though employer endorsement is required if UAN is not Aadhaar-linked. In both cases, supporting documents like PAN, Aadhaar, proof of exit from the last employment, and bank details are essential.
Eligibility Criteria for PF Withdrawal by NRIs
NRIs can withdraw their Provident Fund (PF) under specific circumstances, depending on whether the move abroad is temporary or permanent2.
Temporary Relocation (SSA Countries)
If you are posted to a country that has a Social Security Agreement (SSA) with India, you can apply for a Certificate of Coverage (CoC) from EPFO. This exempts you from social security contributions in the host country while your PF account in India continues, provided your employer keeps contributing to the scheme.
Temporary Relocation (Non-SSA Countries)
If you move to a country without an SSA, you will need to contribute to both the host country’s system and continue your PF contributions in India unless employer arrangement exempts you.
Permanent Relocation Abroad
If you move overseas permanently, you can withdraw your entire PF corpus immediately. To do this, your KYC must be completed on the UAN portal (Aadhaar, PAN, bank details, mobile number), your PF balance should be consolidated into the last active account, and your employer must update your exit date. The withdrawn amount will be credited to your Indian savings or NRO account.
Taxability of PF Withdrawal for NRIs
For NRIs, the tax treatment of Provident Fund withdrawals depends largely on the length of service. Withdrawals made after completing 5 years of continuous service are exempt from tax3. However, if you withdraw before completing 5 years of service, there would be a TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) on the same. The TDS rules are as follows –
1. Withdrawal amount is below ₹50,0003 – No TDS applicable but the withdrawn amount is
taxable if within the income tax bracket 2. Withdrawal amount is more than ₹50,0003 – TDS of 10% is applicable with PAN and 30%
without PAN
To prevent double taxation, NRIs can avail benefits under the Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement (DTAA) between India and their country of residence3.
Non-resident members should also note that a surcharge and 4% cess are added to the TDS. The surcharge varies by income: nil up to ₹50 lakh, 10% from ₹50 lakh to ₹1 crore, 15% from ₹1 crore to ₹2 crore, 25% from ₹2 crore to ₹5 crore, and 37% above ₹5 crore4 for FY2021-22.
Key Takeaways
1. If you permanently relocate abroad and cease employment in India, you can withdraw your
EPF balance immediately without any waiting period1.
2. If you are going abroad temporarily for work, you can keep your EPF account activated.
3. If you are temporarily relocating to a country having an SSA (Social Security Agreement) with
India, you would be exempted from contributing to the foreign country’s social security scheme2.
4. For non-SSA countries, you have to contribute to your EPF account in India and also to the
social security scheme of the country you move to2.
5. For NRIs, PF withdrawal is taxable if done before completing five years of continuous service.
6. Withdrawals made after five years are tax-free in India.
7. A TDS is also applicable on withdrawals made before 5 years if the amount is more than
₹50,000
8. NRIs can use DTAA provisions to reduce their tax liability or claim credit in their country of
residence.
Conclusion
Managing Provident Fund withdrawals as an NRI requires a clear understanding of both eligibility and taxation rules. Withdrawals made after five years of continuous service remain tax-free, but early withdrawals attract TDS and may increase your tax burden if not planned carefully.
By ensuring your KYC is updated, PAN is linked to Aadhaar, and DTAA benefits are properly claimed, you can minimise deductions and maximise the value of your savings. With proper planning, your PF corpus can serve as a strong financial cushion even after you move abroad.
FAQs
1. Can NRIs claim tax exemptions on PF withdrawal under DTAA?
Yes. NRIs can claim benefits under the Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement (DTAA) between India and their country of residence3.
2. What is the TDS rate on PF withdrawal for NRIs?
For NRIs, the TDS rate on Provident Fund withdrawal depends on the length of service and PAN status. If the withdrawal is made before completing five years of continuous service, TDS is deducted at 10% if the PAN is linked and 30% if it isn’t3.
On the other hand, if the PF is withdrawn after five years of service, no TDS is applied.
3. How can NRIs avoid TDS on PF withdrawal?
TDS is applicable only on withdrawals made before 5 years, and the amount is more than ₹50,000. For lower amounts and if the withdrawal is after 5 years of service, no TDS is applicable3.
4. What documents are required to claim tax benefits on PF withdrawal?
Here are the documents that are needed to claim tax benefits on PF withdrawal3 -
● PAN Card ● Aadhaar ● UAN details or EPF passbook if UAN is not allotted ● Bank account details which can be furnished through a cancelled cheque (Indian savings or NRO) ● Proof of date of birth ● Proof of local Indian address ● Proof of last employment exit ● Marriage certificate for females if applicable
5. Does the amount of PF withdrawal affect its taxability for NRIs?
Yes, the amount of PF withdrawal is important for TDS implications. A TDS is applicable if you withdraw the EPF before completing 5 years of service and if the amount is more than ₹50,0003.
